page: Microsoft Azure DC24s_v3 is a member of the DCSv3 server family, utilizing the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features an x86_64 architecture powered by an Intel Xeon 8370C processor running at 2.8 GHz. This configuration provides 24 dedicated physical cores and 24 vCPUs, with a 1.0 thread-per-core ratio. The server is equipped with 192.0 GB of system memory, yielding 8.0 GB of RAM per core. It does not include local storage or integrated GPUs, though it is Premium Storage capable for external disk attachments.

In benchmark evaluations, the DC24s_v3 demonstrates solid mid-range performance. The single-core stress-ng CPU performance is recorded at 1897.99, while the multi-core score reaches 45527.64. Memory bandwidth tests show 2531.67 GB/sec for cached small-block reads and 164.61 GB/sec for uncached large-block reads, alongside a write speed of 64.58 GB/sec. The system performs strongly in specific tasks, such as Geekbench image processing with a score of 15333.0 and small-model LLM inference, achieving 2359.87 tokens/sec for prompt processing and 306.23 tokens/sec for text generation with a 135M parameter model. However, performance scales down on larger workloads, with large-model text generation dropping to a weak 2.74 tokens/sec.

Given its 1:8 vCPU-to-RAM ratio, the DC24s_v3 offers a memory-optimized resource profile without the overhead of bundled local storage or GPU hardware. This makes it a qualitatively cost-efficient option for memory-intensive workloads that rely on network-attached storage. The server is well-suited for database operations, as evidenced by its Passmark database score of 21057.23 and Redis SET performance of 46.21 million operations per second. Other appropriate workloads include software compilation, text processing, and moderate-scale web serving.
description: Microsoft Azure DC24s_v3 is a memory-optimized server from the DCSv3 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features an Intel Xeon 8370C x86_64 processor with 24 dedicated physical cores and 24 vCPUs operating at 2.8 GHz. The system is equipped with 192.0 GB of RAM, providing a 1:8 vCPU-to-memory ratio, but contains no local storage or GPUs. Benchmark results place the server in the average tier for most compute tasks, with a Passmark CPU score of 41766.88 and a multi-core Geekbench score of 15764.0. It exhibits strong performance in image processing and small-model LLM inference, while larger model inference remains a bottleneck. This hardware profile is qualitatively cost-effective for memory-intensive applications relying on network storage, making it suitable for database operations, Redis caching, and software compilation.
og_description: Microsoft Azure DC24s_v3 is a memory-optimized server featuring 24 dedicated Intel Xeon 8370C cores, 192 GB of RAM, and no local storage, designed for database and memory-intensive workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure DC24s_v3 is an x86_64 server with 24 dedicated Intel Xeon cores and 192 GB RAM, optimized for memory-intensive workloads.
tagline: A memory-optimized x86_64 platform featuring twenty-four dedicated Intel Xeon cores and one hundred ninety-two gigabytes of RAM.
bullet_points: Intel Xeon 8370C processor with 24 dedicated physical cores and vCPUs
192 GB of system memory providing a 1:8 vCPU-to-RAM ratio
No local storage or GPU, optimized for network-attached storage
Strong performance in image processing and small-model LLM inference
Suitable for database operations, Redis caching, and software compilation
